name: decidr

prompt_definitions.name: initiative_planning
prompt_definitions.description: Guided strategic interview to plan and scaffold a new initiative with projects, bridges, decisions, tasks, and documents. Acts as a professional strategist helping extract maximum value.

arguments.name: initiative_idea
arguments.description: Optional starting description of the initiative idea

prompt_definitions.name: fix_generation
prompt_definitions.description: Generate a code fix prompt from a GitHub issue with full DecidR context. Provides project, task, and decision context for the issue.

arguments.name: issue_ref_id
arguments.description: The issue ref ID to generate a fix prompt for

prompt_definitions.name: review_generation
prompt_definitions.description: Generate a PR review prompt with full DecidR context. Provides issue, project, and decision context for reviewing external PRs.

arguments.name: pr_artifact_id
arguments.description: The PR artifact ID to generate a review prompt for

prompt_definitions.name: github_pr_lifecycle
prompt_definitions.description: Behavioral runbook for agents participating in the GitHub PR lifecycle governed by DecidR. Covers coder and reviewer role paths, failure modes, and scope boundaries. Fetch and follow before taking any action on a GitHub issue or PR linked to a DecidR entity.

prompt_definitions.name: governance_lifecycle
prompt_definitions.description: Canonical DecidR governance lifecycle runbook. Fetch before creating, proposing, approving, staging, implementing, leaving discovery/planning for implementation, or otherwise moving governed decisions through solo-builder or team workflows.

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: Optional governance mode: solo_builder or team

prompt_definitions.name: governance_checkin
prompt_definitions.description: Manual DecidR governance check-in workflow for pending approvals, assigned reviews, action items, and staged deployment review reminders.

prompt_definitions.name: planning_decision_governance
prompt_definitions.description: Compatibility alias for the DecidR governance lifecycle runbook. Prefer governance_lifecycle for new workflows.

plugin_rules: When the user asks for action items, to-do items, pending work, or what needs attention: call my_action_items and push to decidr_list. Never open decidr_dashboard for these requests.
When the user asks for a DecidR governance check-in, pending approvals, approval reminders, or assigned decision reviews, fetch governance_checkin and follow it.

When the user accepts a durable choice, agreed plan, meaningful discovery, agreed finding, created artifact, persistent object, or release/deploy/commit proof, follow installed DecidR governance lifecycle and work logging startup rules first even if the user did not mention DecidR. Use provided DecidR tools; fetch get_plugin_docs or governance_lifecycle only for schemas or deeper detail.
When using DecidR search, list, or detail tools only for agent discovery, keep results private. Push decidr_list only when the user explicitly asks to see results or details.

When the user asks for a timeline, roadmap, schedule, portfolio timing, or how initiatives shape up over time: push decidr_timeline directly. Do not call list tools first; the renderer fetches its own data.

During init_session, DecidR may provide plugin_contexts.decidr.recentDecisions as current-user decision breadcrumbs from the last 24 hours. Treat them as hints only; call get_decision for a relevant match before implementation.

startup_rules.title: DecidR Team Governance Lifecycle Runtime
startup_rules.description: Setup-configured native startup rule for team approval governance lifecycle behavior.
startup_rules.rule:
Run when the user or work establishes durable project memory, not only when DecidR is named. Triggers include an accepted plan, selected option, agreed finding, meaningful discovery, persistent architecture/product/process choice, created project object, follow-up task, or commit/release/deploy proof. Do this without waiting for the user to ask for governance instructions.

Mode: team approvals.

Provided Tools:
- Use the DecidR tools exposed in this session for search, decisions, tasks, documents, audit evidence, lifecycle transitions, and `governance_lifecycle` when deeper schema detail is needed.
- If names differ by agent, use the provided DecidR Browse, Create & Update, Documents tool groups rather than direct HTTP calls.

Before writing:
- Confirm DecidR is available.
- Search existing initiatives, projects, decisions, tasks, and documents.
- Use active organization members only for owner/implementer fields.
- Use review before ambiguous, destructive, cross-org, high-impact, customer/production-visible, hard-to-undo, major lifecycle-transition, or row-level batch writes.

Do:
- Durable choice, accepted plan, agreed finding, meaningful discovery, tradeoff, architecture path, or persistent behavior -> standard decision or PLAN update.
- Small already-completed rationale -> CATCH_UP decision.
- Execution follow-up -> task.
- Commit/release/deploy proof -> lifecycle document or audit evidence.
- Keep new standard decisions DRAFT until a PLAN document version exists.
- Before governed implementation, save PLAN with `save_decision_document_version`, move through PROPOSED -> APPROVED, then begin unless the user explicitly overrides.
- When implementation starts, move approved work to IN_PROGRESS when allowed and link execution tasks.
- Built or validating work -> save STAGED version and move to STAGED.
- Production-equivalent merge, deploy, release, or committed operational proof -> save IMPLEMENTED version and move to IMPLEMENTED.

Examples:
- User says "let's proceed with option B" -> search, create/update a standard decision, save PLAN before implementation.
- A discovery finding becomes the basis for future work -> capture it as a decision or PLAN update.
- PR merged or installer released -> save IMPLEMENTED proof and move matching decision to IMPLEMENTED.
- Unsure parent project or cross-org target -> use review before writing.

startup_rules.title: DecidR Solo Governance Lifecycle Runtime
startup_rules.description: Setup-configured native startup rule for solo-builder governance lifecycle behavior.
startup_rules.rule:
Run when the user or work establishes durable project memory, not only when DecidR is named. Triggers include an accepted plan, selected option, agreed finding, meaningful discovery, persistent architecture/product/process choice, created project object, follow-up task, or commit/release/deploy proof. Do this without waiting for the user to ask for governance instructions.

Mode: solo builder.

Provided Tools:
- Use the DecidR tools exposed in this session for search, decisions, tasks, documents, audit evidence, lifecycle transitions, and `governance_lifecycle` when deeper schema detail is needed.
- If names differ by agent, use the provided DecidR Browse, Create & Update, Documents tool groups rather than direct HTTP calls.

Before writing:
- Confirm DecidR is available.
- Search existing initiatives, projects, decisions, tasks, and documents.
- Use active organization members only for owner/implementer fields.
- Use review before ambiguous, destructive, cross-org, high-impact, customer/production-visible, hard-to-undo, major lifecycle-transition, or row-level batch writes.

Do:
- Durable choice, accepted plan, agreed finding, meaningful discovery, tradeoff, architecture path, or persistent behavior -> standard decision or PLAN update.
- Small already-completed rationale -> CATCH_UP decision.
- Execution follow-up -> task.
- Commit/release/deploy proof -> lifecycle document or audit evidence.
- Keep new standard decisions DRAFT until a PLAN document version exists.
- Before implementation, save PLAN with `save_decision_document_version`. In solo mode, a clear accepted plan, selected direction, agreed finding, or explicit user request is enough to proceed unless user/org policy asks for team approval.
- Transition only through allowed DecidR status changes.
- Built or validating work -> save STAGED version and move to STAGED.
- Production-equivalent merge, deploy, release, or committed operational proof -> save IMPLEMENTED version and move to IMPLEMENTED.

Examples:
- User says "let's proceed with option B" -> search, create/update a standard decision, save PLAN, then implement.
- Meaningful discovery accepted as true -> capture it as a decision or PLAN update before it drives work.
- Release shipped or commit pushed to live branch -> save IMPLEMENTED proof and move matching decision to IMPLEMENTED.
- Small finished rationale with no full lifecycle needed -> create/update a catch-up decision.

startup_rules.title: DecidR Work Logging Runtime
startup_rules.description: Setup-configured native startup rule for always-on DecidR work logging policy.

setup_questions.question: Do you plan to use DecidR to collaborate with and get approvals from shared decisions with other teammates?
setup_questions.description: This chooses the default lifecycle mode agents use when DecidR governance work does not specify a mode.

options.label: Yes - team approvals
options.description: Use discovery -> PROPOSED -> APPROVED -> implementation -> STAGED for testing/non-prod validation -> IMPLEMENTED after production-equivalent push, merge, deployment, or release.
options.persisted_rule: DecidR governance mode: team approvals. Run governance when the user accepts a durable plan, option, finding, discovery, object, task, or lifecycle proof, even without DecidR wording. Use provided DecidR tools. Short path: search first -> DRAFT + PLAN -> PROPOSED -> APPROVED -> IN_PROGRESS -> STAGED + STAGED doc -> IMPLEMENTED + IMPLEMENTED proof. Review-gate risky or ambiguous writes.

options.label: No - solo builder
options.description: Use discovery -> accepted decision artifact -> implementation -> STAGED for testing/non-prod validation -> IMPLEMENTED after production-equivalent push, merge, deployment, or release.
options.persisted_rule: DecidR governance mode: solo builder. Run governance when the user accepts a durable plan, option, finding, discovery, object, task, or lifecycle proof, even without DecidR wording. Use provided DecidR tools. Short path: search first -> DRAFT + PLAN -> implement after accepted plan/request/finding -> STAGED + STAGED doc -> IMPLEMENTED + IMPLEMENTED proof. Review-gate risky or ambiguous writes.

setup_questions.question: How should agents log DecidR work by default?
setup_questions.description: This controls the local always-on DecidR work logging startup rule for future agent sessions.
setup_questions.guidance: Explain that DecidR logs durable work to decisions first: accepted plans, created artifacts, discoveries, small completed rationale, execution follow-up, verification proof, and release/deploy evidence. Init-session decision breadcrumbs are discovery hints only.

example_outputs.auto_log_confident: A clear low-risk accepted plan, finding, discovery, created artifact, verification result, or plugin setup change logs directly after search. If no exact parent matches but product/company relevance is clear, agents choose the closest project or initiative and log there instead of skipping.
example_outputs.review_first: Agents keep durable-work logging active, but present proposed formal writes for review before creating or updating DecidR records.
example_outputs.capture_only: Agents do not write DecidR records automatically. They summarize candidate durable memory and ask before creating or updating formal records.

options.label: Auto-log confident work
options.description: Search first, then directly log clear low-risk work. If no exact parent matches, use the closest relevant product/company project or initiative instead of skipping.
options.persisted_rule:
Run after meaningful work, at milestones/end-turns, and whenever the user accepts or confirms durable project memory: an agreed plan, selected option, agreed finding, meaningful discovery, created artifact or persistent object, task, verification proof, commit, release, deployment, or lifecycle proof.

Policy: auto-log confident work.

Provided Tools:
- Use the DecidR tools exposed in this session for search, decisions, tasks, documents, audit evidence, and lifecycle transitions.
- If tool names differ by agent, use the provided DecidR tool groups rather than direct HTTP calls.

Before writing:
- Confirm DecidR is available.
- Search existing initiatives, projects, decisions, tasks, and documents.
- Prefer an exact matching decision or task when one exists. Otherwise use the same project or initiative. If no exact parent matches but the work is relevant to the general product, company, or organization involved, choose the closest matching project or initiative and log there. Do not skip DecidR logging solely because the parent is inferred.
- Proceed directly when organization, closest parent, record type, evidence, and impact are clear and low-risk.
- Use review before ambiguous, destructive, cross-org, high-impact, customer/production-visible, major lifecycle-transition, hard-to-undo, or row-level batch writes.

Decision-first mapping:
- Durable choice, accepted plan, agreed finding, meaningful discovery, created artifact, tradeoff, architecture direction, approval-worthy path, or persistent behavior -> standard decision or PLAN update.
- Small already-completed rationale -> catch-up decision.
- Execution-only follow-up -> task.
- Commit, release, deploy, verification result, or operational proof -> lifecycle document or audit evidence.
- Temporary handoff only -> no DecidR record unless the user asks to log it.

Do:
- Log meaningful work unless the user explicitly says not to log, not to write DecidR records, or chooses a manual logging policy.
- At checkpoints, explicitly decide what DecidR decision, task, lifecycle document, or audit evidence should receive the work log before answering or moving on.
- When using an inferred closest parent, state the inferred parent and why it was chosen in the decision rationale/evidence so the record can be corrected later.
- Use governance lifecycle rules for PLAN/STAGED/IMPLEMENTED and `save_decision_document_version` snapshots.

Examples:
- User says "yes, proceed with that plan" -> search, create/update the matching decision, save PLAN.
- An agent creates a durable artifact, implementation plan, release note, generated document, or package -> log it to the matching decision or closest relevant project/initiative.
- A meaningful discovery is accepted as the basis for future work -> capture it as a decision or PLAN update.
- Release published -> save IMPLEMENTED proof on the matching decision.
- Exact parent project unclear but product/company relevance is clear -> create or update the decision under the closest matching project or initiative and note the inferred parent.
- Closest parent ambiguous or risky -> use review before writing.

options.label: Review first
options.description: Keep DecidR logging always-on, but review formal writes before creating or updating records.
options.persisted_rule:
Run after meaningful work, at milestones/end-turns, and whenever the user accepts or confirms durable project memory: an agreed plan, selected option, agreed finding, meaningful discovery, created artifact or persistent object, task, verification proof, commit, release, deployment, or lifecycle proof.

Policy: review first.

Provided Tools:
- Use the DecidR tools exposed in this session for search, decisions, tasks, documents, audit evidence, and lifecycle transitions.
- If tool names differ by agent, use the provided DecidR tool groups rather than direct HTTP calls.

Before writing:
- Confirm DecidR is available.
- Search existing initiatives, projects, decisions, tasks, and documents.
- Prefer an exact matching decision or task when one exists. Otherwise use the same project or initiative. If no exact parent matches but the work is relevant to the general product, company, or organization involved, propose the closest matching project or initiative for review instead of skipping.
- Present formal DecidR writes in review before creating/updating records.

Decision-first mapping:
- Durable choice, accepted plan, agreed finding, meaningful discovery, created artifact, tradeoff, architecture direction, approval-worthy path, or persistent behavior -> standard decision or PLAN update.
- Small already-completed rationale -> catch-up decision.
- Execution-only follow-up -> task.
- Commit, release, deploy, verification result, or operational proof -> lifecycle document or audit evidence.
- Temporary handoff only -> no DecidR record unless the user asks to log it.

Do:
- Review table rows should show target, action, reason, evidence/ref, and risk.
- Execute accepted rows only.
- Use governance lifecycle rules for PLAN/STAGED/IMPLEMENTED and `save_decision_document_version` snapshots.

options.label: Manual only
options.description: Do not write DecidR records automatically; summarize candidate durable memory and ask before formal logging.
options.persisted_rule:
Run after meaningful work, at milestones/end-turns, and whenever the user accepts or confirms durable project memory: an agreed plan, selected option, agreed finding, meaningful discovery, created artifact or persistent object, task, verification proof, commit, release, deployment, or lifecycle proof.

Policy: manual logging only.

Do:
- Confirm DecidR is available before proposing DecidR writes.
- Summarize candidate durable memory in chat and ask before creating, updating, or lifecycle-transitioning DecidR decisions, tasks, documents, or audit evidence.
- When user approves formal logging, search existing initiatives, projects, decisions, tasks, and documents first.
- Prefer an exact matching decision or task when one exists. Otherwise use the same project or initiative. If no exact parent matches but the work is relevant to the general product, company, or organization involved, propose the closest matching project or initiative instead of skipping.
- Use decision-first mapping and governance lifecycle rules.
- Use `save_decision_document_version` for PLAN, STAGED, and IMPLEMENTED snapshots.

Examples:
- End of coding session -> summarize candidate decision/task/doc updates; do not write automatically.
- User says "log this formally" -> search, propose/create the formal DecidR record.
- Release proof exists but no approval to log -> explain what could be logged and wait.

registry_index.summary: Decision governance — initiatives, projects, decisions, tasks, and bridges with approval workflows

tool_groups.name: Action Items
tool_groups.hint: Prioritized action items: pending approvals, assigned tasks, stalled decisions. BACKLOG decisions/tasks are intentionally non-actionable and stay out of Next Steps. Push results to decidr_list.

tool_groups.name: Browse
tool_groups.hint: List and search initiatives, projects, decisions, tasks, bridges, and audit ledger events. Filter by BACKLOG to inspect future/non-actionable work. Use private discovery before implementation starts and before commit governance; update matching work to STAGED only after built/tested work exists, and to IMPLEMENTED only after production-equivalent release.

tool_groups.name: Details
tool_groups.hint: Get full entity details including status, approvals, linked documents, audit links, and revision history

tool_groups.name: Create & Update
tool_groups.hint: Create and modify initiatives, projects, decisions, tasks, bridges, audit events, and audit categories. Before implementation starts after discovery/planning, follow the installed DecidR governance lifecycle startup rule and verify or propose the governing decision/task. Fetch governance_lifecycle for deeper detail when needed. Clear low-risk decision/doc/task updates can proceed directly; use MCPViews review for significant, ambiguous, destructive, high-impact, or hard-to-undo changes.

tool_groups.name: Organization
tool_groups.hint: Create organizations and manage members. Use list_members before assigning people; invite missing people by email.

tool_groups.name: Documents
tool_groups.hint: Link and unlink documents on projects, decisions, and tasks; search LudFlow docs; save LudFlow versions or external evidence for decision lifecycles; find entities by document

tool_groups.name: Archive & Restore
tool_groups.hint: Soft-delete (archive) entities or restore previously archived ones

tool_groups.name: GitHub
tool_groups.hint: GitHub integration — Ludflow-backed inbound sync plus DecidR PAT-backed outbound writes

tool_rules.my_action_items: Returns prioritized action items as entity refs. Draft, approved, in-progress, and staged decisions appear when the user is owner or implementer; proposed decisions appear when awaiting the user's review. ALWAYS push results to decidr_list — the renderer fetches full data from the API. Never use decidr_dashboard for action item requests. Drill into individual items with get_decision/get_task.
tool_rules.list_initiatives: Use to discover all initiatives. Push to decidr_list. Use format=compact to save tokens. The standalone graph view is currently hidden from this plugin line.
tool_rules.list_projects: Use to discover projects. Push to decidr_list. Filter by status to find active work. Use initiative_id to filter by initiative.
tool_rules.list_decisions: Use to discover decisions. Filter status=BACKLOG to inspect future/non-actionable decisions. No auto-push: keep discovery-only calls private. Push to decidr_list only when the user explicitly asked to see/list/browse decisions. Call get_decision for full details including approval progress.
tool_rules.list_audit_events: Use to review a project's universal audit ledger, especially for 'what happened' questions. Push to decidr_list when the user explicitly asked to see the ledger or audit trail.
tool_rules.list_audit_categories: Use to discover or autocomplete audit event categories. No auto-push unless the user asked to see categories.
tool_rules.list_tasks: Use to discover tasks. Filter status=BACKLOG to inspect future/non-actionable tasks. Push to decidr_list. Filter by assigned_to_me=true for the user's work.
tool_rules.list_bridges: Use to discover project and decision bridges. Push to decidr_list.
tool_rules.get_initiative: Get full initiative details. Push to decidr_list for detail view.
tool_rules.get_project: Get full project details including task/decision/bridge counts. Push to decidr_list.
tool_rules.get_decision: Get full decision details including approval progress and linked documents. No auto-push: keep discovery-only calls private. Push to decidr_list only when the user explicitly asked to see/open the decision details. Use BACKLOG for future/non-actionable decisions that should stay out of Next Steps. Draft standard decisions need a linked Ludflow document with a PLAN version before leaving DRAFT. STAGED is only for already-built/configured work in test/review or non-production validation, not for accepted plans, and requires a STAGED document version. Check list_entity_documents for supporting context and use allowedTransitions to choose the next valid status. For standard decisions, IMPLEMENTED is reserved for live or production-equivalent release and requires an IMPLEMENTED document version: production deployment, deployment-codebase migration, push/merge to the repo live release branch (main/master/prod/production when applicable), or an installable versioned release. CATCH_UP decisions are the exception and are directly IMPLEMENTED for post-implementation capture.
tool_rules.get_audit_event: Get a full audit event with payload, source context, linked decisions, URL links, and visible revision history. Push to decidr_list when the user asked to inspect the audit record.
tool_rules.get_task: Get full task details. BACKLOG tasks are future/non-actionable and should stay out of Next Steps. If status is TODO or IN_PROGRESS, user can use update_task with status: DONE. Push to decidr_list.
tool_rules.get_bridge: Get full bridge details including project or decision endpoints and linked decisions. Push to decidr_list.
tool_rules.search: Use when user describes something by name or keyword. No auto-push: keep agent-discovery searches private. Push results to decidr_list only when the user explicitly asked to see/search/browse matching decisions or entities. Offer to drill into specific results with get_* tools.
tool_rules.create_initiative: Create a new initiative. Returns full created entity. Consider creating projects under it next.
tool_rules.create_project: Create a new project. Pass initiative_id to link to an initiative. Before passing owner_id or member_ids, call list_members and use only active member user IDs; pending invitation IDs are not assignable. Returns full created entity. Consider adding decisions or tasks next.
tool_rules.create_decision: Create a new standard decision. Requires entity_type and matching parent ID. Decisions default to DRAFT status and owner_id defaults to the creator when omitted; before passing owner_id or implementer_id, call list_members and use only active member user IDs. Pass status BACKLOG for future/non-actionable decisions that should stay out of Next Steps. After human-accepted discovery, create/update the decision and link the discovery artifact, then transition to PROPOSED for review when needed. Use STAGED only when recording already-built/configured work that is in test/review or non-production validation, not for accepted plans. Use create_catch_up_decision instead for small already-implemented work that needs post-implementation rationale capture without the full standard workflow. Use IMPLEMENTED only after live or production-equivalent release: production deployment, deployment-codebase migration, push/merge to the repo live release branch (main/master/prod/production when applicable), or an installable versioned release. When creating a missing governance record from implementation discovery, proceed directly for clear low-risk single decisions and accompanying doc links/edits after search/context checks; use MCPViews review only for significant, ambiguous, destructive, high-impact, or hard-to-undo changes.
tool_rules.create_catch_up_decision: Create an IMPLEMENTED catch-up decision for small work that already happened and needs governance/rationale captured after the fact. Requires title, description/rationale, entity_type, and matching parent ID. Optional implemented_at records when the work actually happened. Do not use for active approval workflow or live status transitions.
tool_rules.create_audit_event: Create a factual project-ledger audit event. Admins may create new free-form categories. external_reference_id plus created_by_client is idempotent.
tool_rules.create_task: Create a new task. Returns full created entity. Before passing assignee_id, call list_members and use only an active member user ID; pending invitation IDs are not assignable. Pass status BACKLOG for future/non-actionable work that should stay out of Next Steps. Use for lightweight implementation follow-up that did not need a decision approval flow, under the relevant project or existing decision. When creating a missing task from implementation discovery, proceed directly for clear low-risk tasks after search/context checks; use MCPViews review only for significant, ambiguous, destructive, high-impact, or hard-to-undo changes.
tool_rules.create_bridge: Create a bridge between two projects or two decisions. Returns full created entity.
tool_rules.update_initiative: Update initiative fields. Returns full updated entity.
tool_rules.update_project: Update project fields and/or transition status. Before changing owner_id or project members, call list_members and use only active member user IDs; pending invitation IDs are not assignable. Returns full updated entity.
tool_rules.update_decision: Update standard decision fields, responsibilities, and/or transition status. Before changing owner_id or implementer_id, call list_members and use only active member user IDs. Use action param for approve/reject/supersede/unsupersede. Use BACKLOG for future/non-actionable decisions that should stay out of Next Steps. Draft decisions require a linked Ludflow PLAN document version before transitioning to PROPOSED or STAGED. Before implementation starts after discovery/planning, verify or propose the governing decision/task; do not mark accepted plans as STAGED. Move matching decisions to STAGED only after the work is built/configured and in test/review or non-production validation and a STAGED document version has been saved. Move matching STAGED decisions to IMPLEMENTED only after live or production-equivalent release and an IMPLEMENTED document version has been saved: production deployment, deployment-codebase migration, push/merge to the repo live release branch (main/master/prod/production when applicable), or an installable versioned release. If a draft/backlog decision is actually small already-implemented catch-up work, use mark_decision_as_catch_up instead. Returns the full updated entity. Field updates are applied before status transition.
tool_rules.mark_decision_as_catch_up: Convert an existing DRAFT or BACKLOG decision into an IMPLEMENTED catch-up decision. Use when the record captures small already-implemented work after the fact and a full standard document-gated workflow would add noise. Provide description if the existing decision lacks rationale, and optional implemented_at for the actual implementation time.
tool_rules.update_decision_stage_time: Correct when a decision stage/status actually occurred without changing live decision status. Provide id, status, occurred_at ISO timestamp, and optional reason. Updates the matching STATUS_CHANGED timeline event occurred_at while preserving createdAt as the write/correction audit timestamp. For v1, only the decision creator or organization admins/owners may correct timing.
tool_rules.update_audit_event: Edit an audit event and create visible revision history. Only admins can change occurred_at.
tool_rules.update_task: Update task fields and/or transition status. Before changing assignee_id, call list_members and use only an active member user ID; pending invitation IDs are not assignable. Use BACKLOG for future/non-actionable work that should stay out of Next Steps. Set status to DONE to complete a task. Returns full updated entity.
tool_rules.update_task_stage_time: Correct when a task stage/status actually occurred without changing live task status. Provide id, status, occurred_at ISO timestamp, and optional reason. Updates the matching STATUS_CHANGED timeline event occurred_at while preserving createdAt as the write/correction audit timestamp. For v1, only the task creator or organization admins/owners may correct timing.
tool_rules.update_bridge: Update bridge fields and/or transition status. Returns full updated entity.
tool_rules.create_organization: Create a new organization. The caller becomes OWNER. Use invite_member to invite others. DecidR setup/onboarding agents must pass creation_source: "DECIDR_ONBOARDING" so the starter initiative, project, decision, and Ludflow document are seeded. Other callers should omit creation_source or pass GENERIC.
tool_rules.list_organizations: List organizations the current user belongs to.
tool_rules.list_members: List all active members of the current organization. Use returned active member user IDs for owner_id, implementer_id, member_ids, assignee_id, and reviewer_id fields.
tool_rules.list_member_invites: List pending member invitations for the current organization. Pending invitation IDs are not assignable user IDs; call list_members after acceptance before assigning the person to records.
tool_rules.invite_member: Invite a new team member to the current organization by email. Creates a pending invitation and sends the invite email. Owners/admins only. Defaults role to MEMBER and target_product to DECIDR. When inviting a referenced person, leave owner/assignee/reviewer/member fields unset until they accept unless the user chooses an active member.
tool_rules.manage_member: Invite, update role, or remove organization members. Prefer invite_member for new invitations. Use action: add with email to send an invite, update_role with user_id+role to change role, remove with user_id to remove a member, cancel_invite with invitation_id to cancel a pending invite, or resend_invite with invitation_id to resend the email. Pending invitation IDs are not assignable user IDs.
tool_rules.manage_audit_category: Admin-only audit category management. Categories can be created, disabled, or merged; they are not hard-deleted after use.
tool_rules.link_decision_audit_event: Link or unlink an audit event and decision. Linked audit events appear in decision slideouts and linked decisions appear in audit event slideouts.
tool_rules.replace_decisions: Approve a replacement decision, reject one or more originals, record replacement relationships, and copy linked audit events forward.
tool_rules.link_document: Attach a document to any entity. Use type URL for passive links, LUDFLOW for LudFlow documents, and EXTERNAL for provider-neutral external docs such as Notion. Search LudFlow docs first with search_ludflow_documents when using LUDFLOW.
tool_rules.list_entity_documents: List all documents linked to an entity. Essential context before approving decisions.
tool_rules.unlink_document: Unlink a document/file from a project, decision, or task. Use list_entity_documents first to get the document_id.
tool_rules.search_ludflow_documents: Search LudFlow documents by title. Use results with link_document to attach to DecidR entities.
tool_rules.find_entities_by_ludflow_document: Find all DecidR entities linked to a LudFlow document. Enables bidirectional navigation.
tool_rules.find_entities_by_external_document: Find DecidR entities linked to a provider-neutral external document by provider/external_id and/or URL.
tool_rules.save_decision_document_version: Save a lifecycle-tagged Ludflow document version for a DecidR decision. Use PLAN after accepted discovery/planning, STAGED when implementation is built and validating, and IMPLEMENTED when the live implemented state is captured. Creates and links the first PLAN document when no linked Ludflow document exists; provide document_id when multiple Ludflow docs are linked.
tool_rules.save_decision_document_evidence: Save PLAN/STAGED/IMPLEMENTED lifecycle evidence for an EXTERNAL document already linked to a decision. The backend hashes the snapshot and the evidence can satisfy lifecycle gates.
tool_rules.start_evidence_file_upload: Start a direct LudFlow evidence file upload. Upload bytes to the returned uploadUrl with uploadHeaders, then call complete_evidence_file_upload.
tool_rules.complete_evidence_file_upload: Complete a direct evidence file upload. LudFlow encrypts/stores the final asset and DecidR links it as LUDFLOW evidence with lifecycle metadata.
tool_rules.upload_evidence_file: Small-file base64 shortcut for uploading evidence into LudFlow and linking it as LUDFLOW. Use start/complete for larger files.
tool_rules.list_decision_document_evidence: List provider-neutral external document evidence snapshots captured for a decision.
tool_rules.archive_entity: Soft-delete (archive) an entity. Archived entities are hidden from default lists but can be restored. Use when the user wants to remove or delete an entity.
tool_rules.restore_entity: Restore a previously archived entity. Use when the user wants to undelete or bring back an archived entity.
tool_rules.connect_github: Do NOT call this tool directly with a user-supplied PAT — that leaks the token through the chat. Instead push the decidr_github_auth renderer so the user enters their PAT in the companion's secure form. This PAT is only for outbound GitHub write actions such as create_issue/create_pr/review_pr/merge_pr.
tool_rules.disconnect_github: Disconnect GitHub account from the current org. No auto-push.
tool_rules.list_repos: List DecidR-linked GitHub repos. Push to decidr_list.
tool_rules.get_repo_last_sync: Get Ludflow-backed metadata sync state for a linked repo, including the last sync timestamp. Push to decidr_list.
tool_rules.refresh_repo_metadata: Ask Ludflow to refresh GitHub metadata for a linked repo. Use when the user wants a fresh inbound sync. No auto-push.
tool_rules.link_repo_to_project: Link a GitHub repo to a project so Ludflow-synced issues and pull requests appear on that project. No auto-push.
tool_rules.create_issue: Create a GitHub issue using the caller's DecidR PAT connection and record the created issue in DecidR. No auto-push.
tool_rules.list_issues: List synced GitHub issues. Push to decidr_list.
tool_rules.link_issue: Link a GitHub issue to a DecidR entity. No auto-push.
tool_rules.unlink_issue: Unlink a GitHub issue from a DecidR entity. No auto-push.
tool_rules.list_issue_links: List entity links for a GitHub issue. Push to decidr_list.
tool_rules.create_pr: Create a GitHub pull request using the caller's DecidR PAT connection and record the resulting PR artifact in DecidR. Before passing reviewer_id, call list_members and use only an active member user ID; pending invitation IDs are not assignable. No auto-push.
tool_rules.list_prs: List PR artifacts. Push to decidr_list.
tool_rules.review_pr: Record the current user as reviewer in DecidR and, for APPROVED/CHANGES_REQUESTED, post the corresponding review to GitHub. No auto-push.
tool_rules.merge_pr: Merge a GitHub pull request and mark the DecidR PR artifact as MERGED. No auto-push.
tool_rules.generate_fix_prompt: Generate a code fix prompt from issue context. Returns text. No auto-push.
tool_rules.generate_review_prompt: Generate a PR review prompt. Returns text. No auto-push.
tool_rules.manage_repo_admin: Add or remove repo admin. No auto-push.
tool_rules.get_init_context: Read-only init-session provider used by MCPViews. Returns current-user decision breadcrumbs from the last 24 hours as id, title, and description only. Treat as private discovery; call get_decision for a relevant match before implementation.

renderer_definitions.name: decidr_list
renderer_definitions.description: Universal entity renderer — displays lists, details, search results, and action items with slide-out panels for navigation
renderer_definitions.rule: Use for user-facing entity list, detail, and search views. Do not push for agent-only discovery calls. Always use ref-only mode — pass entity type + ID, never full payloads.
renderer_definitions.data_hint: IMPORTANT: Prefer ref-only mode. Pass entity references — the renderer fetches full data from the API. Format: { entities: [{ type: 'initiative'|'project'|'decision'|'audit_event'|'task'|'bridge'|'issue'|'pull_request'|'repo', id: string }], meta: { organization_id: string, result_count: number, tool_name: string } }. The tool_name determines rendering mode: 'list_decisions' or 'list_audit_events' for list view, 'get_decision' or 'get_audit_event' for detail view, 'my_action_items' for action items, 'search' for search results. Entity type names are singular lowercase.

renderer_definitions.name: decidr_dashboard
renderer_definitions.description: User Dashboard with initiative grouping, health bars, project cards, and prioritized action items
renderer_definitions.rule: Push directly to show the User Dashboard — do NOT call my_action_items or any other tool first. The renderer fetches all data from the API. Only pass organization_id. For fast access, push immediately when user asks for the User Dashboard, dashboard, or overview.

renderer_definitions.name: decidr_timeline
renderer_definitions.description: Executive timeline view showing planned initiative trajectory, task due dates, lifecycle milestones, and actual activity over time
renderer_definitions.rule: Push directly when the user asks for a timeline, roadmap, schedule, portfolio timing, or how initiatives shape up over time. Do NOT call list tools first. The renderer fetches all data from the API. Optionally pass organization_id, initiative_ids, and view.
renderer_definitions.invoke_schema: { "type": "object", "properties": { "initiative_ids": { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string" }, "description": "Initiative IDs to scope the timeline view. Omit for all initiatives." }, "view": { "type": "string", "enum": ["all", "initiative"], "description": "Initial timeline scope. Defaults to all." } } }

renderer_definitions.name: decidr_github_auth
renderer_definitions.description: Secure GitHub authentication form — enter PAT directly in the companion without passing through the AI agent
renderer_definitions.rule: Push whenever the user needs to connect a GitHub PAT for outbound GitHub writes OR when a write tool fails with 'GitHub connection not found'. Renders a secure form in the companion — the user's PAT goes straight to DecidR and never passes through the agent. Never ask the user to paste a PAT in chat; push this renderer instead.
renderer_definitions.data_hint: { github_username?: string } — push with empty data for a blank form, or pre-fill the username field. The renderer POSTs the PAT directly to DecidR's /github/connections endpoint; no follow-up connect_github call is needed.

plugin_rule_definitions.rule: DecidR MCPViews auth uses in-app six-digit email-code authentication by default. Use init_session org_tokens to inspect org auth. If target org auth is missing or expired_unrefreshable, call start_plugin_auth with plugin_name 'decidr' and organization_id before retrying. Treat expired_refreshable as connected while MCPViews refreshes it; use auth_flow 'browser' only for advanced OAuth recovery.
Use BACKLOG status for future or intentionally non-actionable decisions and tasks. BACKLOG records should not be treated as Next Steps or immediate action items.

When a user references people by name or email while creating or updating DecidR records, resolve them with list_members before setting user ID fields such as owner_id, implementer_id, member_ids, assignee_id, or reviewer_id. If no active member matches and an email is known, call invite_member and leave assignment fields unset until that invite is accepted unless the user chooses an existing member. Pending invitation IDs are not assignable user IDs. If no email is known, ask for the email or ask which active member should own the work.

When the user asks to see entity details from get_initiative, get_project, get_decision, get_task, or get_bridge, push to decidr_list. Only open decidr_dashboard when the user explicitly asks for the User Dashboard, dashboard, or overview.

When creating, updating, approving, staging, implementing, or logging DecidR-governed decision work, follow any installed DecidR governance lifecycle startup rule and use the persisted governance mode unless the user explicitly chooses another mode. When leaving discovery or planning for governed feature/process implementation, verify or propose the governing decision/task before the first repo edit, packaging command, deployment prep, or external write. Fetch governance_lifecycle only for deeper detail or when no startup rule is available.

When a workflow successfully pushes or merges work to the repository live release branch such as main, master, prod, or production, or publishes an installable versioned release, tag, or package, move confident matching STAGED decisions to IMPLEMENTED. If that target is staging, QA, preview, or pre-production only, keep them STAGED.

When governed implementation work has no confident matching DecidR decision or task, follow the installed DecidR governance lifecycle startup rule and choose the right path: create clear low-risk records directly after search/context checks, but use MCPViews review for significant, ambiguous, destructive, high-impact, or hard-to-undo mutations. Fetch governance_lifecycle only for deeper detail or when no startup rule is available.

Use create_catch_up_decision or mark_decision_as_catch_up for small already-implemented work that needs post-implementation rationale capture without the full standard decision workflow. Catch-up decisions are directly IMPLEMENTED, require a rationale, and should not be used for active approval/status transitions.

For standard decision lifecycle documents, use save_decision_document_version for LudFlow-backed markdown docs, start_evidence_file_upload/complete_evidence_file_upload or upload_evidence_file for LudFlow-backed file evidence, or save_decision_document_evidence for EXTERNAL provider docs: save accepted plans as PLAN, staged implementation state as STAGED, and live implemented state as IMPLEMENTED. Passive URL links do not satisfy lifecycle gates. Uploaded evidence files attach as LUDFLOW document links with lifecycle metadata. Approval remains DecidR status/progress, not document metadata. When creating supporting LudFlow documents for DecidR work, place them in an existing relevant folder structure or, if none exists, in a folder named for the DecidR project; fetch governance_lifecycle only when deeper document-placement detail is needed.

When updating a LudFlow document linked to an implemented DecidR decision, or a LudFlow document that is already published, preserve the existing implemented content and append new findings as a dated addendum unless the user explicitly asks for a rewrite.

Before taking any action on a GitHub issue or PR linked to DecidR with create_issue, create_pr, review_pr, merge_pr, or prompt generation, fetch github_pr_lifecycle and follow it. Inbound GitHub sync is automatic through Ludflow-backed metadata. Use get_repo_last_sync or refresh_repo_metadata when the user asks about freshness, and never reintroduce manual sync or reconciliation instructions.

When a GitHub write tool fails with 'GitHub connection not found' or the user asks to connect a GitHub PAT for outbound writes, push the decidr_github_auth renderer. Do not call connect_github with a PAT collected in chat. For org-level GitHub connection and metadata-sync settings, use the organization settings GitHub section backed by Ludflow, not the PAT flow.

When the user asks what happened on a project or needs an auditable project ledger, use list_audit_events or get_audit_event and push to decidr_list when the user asked to see the results. Audit dashboard, audit report, and graph renderers are currently hidden from this DecidR plugin line; use list/get tools with decidr_list for audit ledger and relationship visibility until those renderers move to future plugin lines.

Use update_decision_stage_time and update_task_stage_time only to correct historical stage occurrence timestamps. They must not be used to transition live status; use update_decision or update_task for actual status changes.

Archive and restore tools are soft-delete workflows. Use them only when the user clearly wants an entity removed or restored, and prefer MCPViews review for destructive, ambiguous, high-impact, or hard-to-undo batches.
